entities.name: ROOT SYSTEM
entities.description: The underground organ system of a plant responsible for anchorage and absorption of water and nutrients

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: Environmental constraint characterized by a lack of sufficient water

entities.name: NUTRIENT DEFICIENCY
entities.description: Environmental constraint characterized by a lack of sufficient essential nutrients

entities.name: TOXIC MINERALS
entities.description: Environmental constraint involving exposure to harmful mineral concentrations in the soil

entities.name: SOIL COMPACTION
entities.description: Physical environmental constraint where soil particles are pressed together, restricting root growth

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of growing agricultural crops for food, feed, or fiber

entities.name: ROOT DEPTH
entities.description: The vertical extent to which roots penetrate the soil profile

entities.name: ROOT ABUNDANCE
entities.description: The density or quantity of roots within a given volume of soil

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to maintain growth and productivity during water-limited conditions

entities.name: PROFUSE ROOTING
entities.description: The development of an extensive and dense root system

entities.name: NUTRIENT CAPTURE
entities.description: The process by which roots acquire essential nutrients from the soil

entities.name: MEMBRANE TRANSPORTERS
entities.description: Proteins located in cell membranes that regulate the movement of ions and molecules

entities.name: SALTS
entities.description: Ionic substances in the soil that can cause osmotic stress or toxicity

entities.name: ROOT HYDRAULICS
entities.description: The physical properties and mechanisms governing the movement of water through the root system

entities.name: WATER UPTAKE
entities.description: The process of absorbing water from the soil into the plant

entities.name: STRESS SIGNALING
entities.description: The biochemical communication pathways that transmit information about environmental stress from roots to other plant organs

entities.name: SHOOT
entities.description: The above-ground portion of the plant, including stems and leaves

entities.name: WATER ABSORPTION
entities.description: The physiological process of taking up water from the environment

entities.name: NUTRIENT ABSORPTION
entities.description: The physiological process of taking up minerals and nutrients from the environment

entities.name: XYLEM VESSELS
entities.description: Specialized tissues that transport water and dissolved minerals from the roots to the shoot

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESS
entities.description: Non-living environmental factors that negatively impact plant growth and development

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: An essential plant nutrient often subject to limitation in various soil types

entities.name: SALT STRESS
entities.description: Stress induced by high concentrations of soluble salts in the soil

entities.name: ROOT STRUCTURE
entities.description: The physical arrangement, morphology, and architecture of the root system

entities.name: NEAR ISOGENIC LINES
entities.description: Genetic lines that are identical except for a small, specific genomic region, used to study the effect of particular genes
